<findings>

[observation] [warning] 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [warning] Bidirectional imports with analyzers-2 (15 in, 7 out) suggest a forced algorithmic split — these zones likely represent a single logical subsystem and should be reviewed for consolidation.
[observation] [info] Cohesion of 0.67 is healthy but 16 entry points create a wide coupling surface; narrowing exports to a facade module would lower downstream coupling.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "analysis-engine" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] Audit archetypes.ts import count across zones — if it is imported by 2+ zones it is already a de-facto cross-cutting hub and should be extracted to the shared-types foundation alongside v1.ts
[suggestion] [info] Group enrich-batch.ts and enrich-config.ts into a src/analyzers/enrichment/ subdirectory — the shared prefix is an implicit sub-module boundary that should be made structural
[pattern] [info] analysis-engine has no outbound imports to cli-infrastructure or branch-work-analytics yet both import heavily from it (12 and 27 crossings respectively) — this unidirectional fan-out is healthy and should be preserved as the package grows
[anti-pattern] [critical] analysis-engine → branch-work-analytics has 6 outbound imports in addition to the 27 inbound, creating a 33-crossing bidirectional pair — the highest cross-zone traffic volume in the package; this volume indicates the two zones share mutable state or type definitions that belong in a shared foundation, not in either consumer zone
[anti-pattern] [warning] claude-client.ts (vendor HTTP adapter) and completion-reader.ts (streaming I/O parser) are infrastructure files embedded in the domain zone; they should be extracted to an infra or adapters layer so the domain zone contains no vendor or transport concerns

</findings>

<insights>

- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- With 16 entry points this zone is the primary public surface of sourcevision; consider whether all entry points need to be exposed or whether a narrower facade would reduce accidental coupling from consumers.
- The bidirectional import relationship with analyzers-2 (15 in, 7 out) indicates the split between these two zones is artificial — merging or introducing a clear sub-module boundary would improve conceptual clarity.
- The callgraph, context, llms-txt, and workspace-crossings files represent distinct output concerns; grouping them under a dedicated output sub-module would make the pipeline stages more auditable.
- Cohesion of 0.67 is healthy but 16 entry points create a wide coupling surface; narrowing exports to a facade module would lower downstream coupling.
- Bidirectional imports with analyzers-2 (15 in, 7 out) suggest a forced algorithmic split — these zones likely represent a single logical subsystem and should be reviewed for consolidation.
- Zone "analysis-engine" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- analysis-engine is the sole zone imported by every other non-config zone, making it the architectural hub of the entire package; this gravity means any breaking change here ripples across all consumers simultaneously
- analysis-engine has no outbound imports to cli-infrastructure or branch-work-analytics yet both import heavily from it (12 and 27 crossings respectively) — this unidirectional fan-out is healthy and should be preserved as the package grows
- claude-client.ts is a vendor-specific HTTP adapter living inside the domain zone — infrastructure (AI vendor coupling) is mixed with domain logic, so any vendor change requires edits inside the zone that should be technology-agnostic
- completion-reader.ts is a streaming-response parser, an infrastructure I/O concern; its presence in analysis-engine means domain logic is structurally coupled to the AI transport layer rather than consuming a clean interface
- The 6 reverse imports from analysis-engine back into branch-work-analytics (analyzers → unit) make this the highest-volume bidirectional pair in the package at 33 total crossings (27+6), yet it has not been flagged with a dedicated resolution plan separate from the three-zone cycle
- claude-client.ts (vendor HTTP adapter) and completion-reader.ts (streaming I/O parser) are infrastructure files embedded in the domain zone; they should be extracted to an infra or adapters layer so the domain zone contains no vendor or transport concerns
- analysis-engine → branch-work-analytics has 6 outbound imports in addition to the 27 inbound, creating a 33-crossing bidirectional pair — the highest cross-zone traffic volume in the package; this volume indicates the two zones share mutable state or type definitions that belong in a shared foundation, not in either consumer zone
- archetypes.ts is typed as [types] but resides in the zone with the widest public surface — if it is already imported by multiple zones it belongs in the shared-types extraction alongside v1.ts and schema/index.ts, not in the domain zone
- The enrich-batch.ts and enrich-config.ts files share an 'enrich-' naming prefix not shared by any other file in this zone — this prefix signals a self-contained sub-concern (AI enrichment) that could be extracted as an enrichment sub-module without touching the rest of the zone
- Audit archetypes.ts import count across zones — if it is imported by 2+ zones it is already a de-facto cross-cutting hub and should be extracted to the shared-types foundation alongside v1.ts
- Group enrich-batch.ts and enrich-config.ts into a src/analyzers/enrichment/ subdirectory — the shared prefix is an implicit sub-module boundary that should be made structural
- [call graph] 997 internal calls, 93 outgoing, 208 incoming (cohesion: 0.91, coupling: 0.09)

</insights>
